content:
When instructed to find a card, a player searches each game area where that card could be found. All game areas are subject to search except: facedown encounter cards in any in-play area, the victory display, and cards removed from the game.

If instructed to 'find and reveal' an already-in-play minion, that player engages that minion and resolves any keywords/triggered abilities from being revealed (e.g. When Revealed); it retains attached cards/tokens and is not considered to be entering play.

• A first player is determined by the players at the start of the game, indicated by the first player token. At the end of the round (step five of the villain phase) the token passes clockwise.
  • If the first player is eliminated, the token passes clockwise immediately.
  • The first player decides eligible targets/actions when unspecified, and resolution order of simultaneous effects.
  • The first player has first opportunity for interrupts and responses at each moment.

When instructed to flip a card, turn it upside down so the faceup side becomes facedown. A foldable 'three-sided' card is considered flipped any time its faceup side changes.

If the new faceup side has the same card type as before, the card retains attached cards, tucked cards, status cards, and tokens; if a different card type, all of these are discarded.

'For each' indicates an effect repeated based on the number of a countable game element. If a 'for each' effect requires a target, it applies to a single target unless it includes a 'choose' instruction (in which case each iteration is a separate instance, even choosing the same target multiple times — the game state updates after each instance and responses can trigger after each).

If another ability modifies a 'for each' effect, that modifier applies to each instance.

Forced is a bold trigger word; if it precedes a triggered ability, initiation is mandatory. 'Forced Interrupt' and 'Forced Response' abilities must resolve when triggering conditions are met. 'Forced Action' abilities can be triggered any time a non-forced action could be, but must resolve before the player phase ends.

  • If a forced ability requires targets and has none valid, it does not initiate (and costs are not paid). Forced interrupts/responses take priority over non-forced ones for the same triggering condition.
  • If two+ forced abilities initiate at the same moment, the first player determines order regardless of who controls them.
  • Each forced ability must resolve as completely as possible before the next triggered by the same condition may initiate.

• A player can be in hero or alter-ego form, indicated by their identity card.
  • Once each round during their turn, a player may change form by flipping their identity card.
  • Only the form changes — sustained damage, status cards, lasting effects, attached/tucked cards, tokens, and readied/exhausted state are retained.
  • If a card ability causes a form change, it doesn't count against the once-per-turn voluntary change.
  • While in hero form, abilities interacting with alter-ego don't interact with identity, and vice versa.
  • Cards with '[type] form' keyword grant additional unique forms (in addition to hero/alter-ego); changing into these doesn't count against the once-per-turn limit but does count as changing form for triggering purposes.
  • Cards with '[type] form only' can only be played/put into play by a player in that form.
Friendly is a blanket term referring to cards the players control.
